$comment: Canonical UAE spend-category taxonomy. This is the shared vocabulary that links a card's reward tiers to a customer's spend vector. Every reward tier and every profile spend bucket MUST use one of these keys. 'international' is NOT a category here: foreign-currency spend is modelled as a separate scalar (international_share) because it cross-cuts every category (you can dine, shop, or fuel abroad). Keeping FX orthogonal avoids double-counting.

categories.label: Transport & toll
categories.examples: Salik, RTA, Nol, taxis, parking, metro

categories.label: Mobile wallet
categories.examples: Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay tokenised spend

categories.label: Government & charity
categories.examples: govt fees, fines, charity, RTA fines

categories.label: Everything else
categories.examples: general retail, fashion, electronics, base spend

notes: mobile_wallet overlaps other categories (a wallet grocery tap is both). When a card rewards mobile_wallet specifically (e.g. Standard Chartered X), the profile should carry a mobile_wallet bucket for the share of spend the customer actually tokenises; the engine treats it as its own bucket to avoid double counting.
international / non-AED spend is the profile field `international_share` (0..1 of total spend), applied on top of the category split.
